stories.title: A Year of Us
stories.description: A quiet, reflective moment between Adachi and Shimamura in the gym—a year after they first met.
A quiet moment in the gym during midsummer heat. Adachi ties Shimamura's hair for the first time.

stories.title: Like Endless Bubbles
stories.description: A quiet, philosophical moment between Hino and Nagafuji after school.

stories.title: Two Flowers
stories.description: Shimamura runs into Adachi's mother at the gym pool. A conversation about names, similarities, and family.

stories.title: Adachi and the Zebra
stories.description: Adachi comes home to find a zebra in her apartment—Yashiro, wearing a zebra costume. A short, whimsical glimpse into their strange bond.

stories.title: Michi, Deuchi, and Hasaka
stories.description: Three friends spend Valentine's Day together at the mall, reflecting on their friendship and what the future holds.

stories.title: A Wish
stories.description: The night before the new school year begins, Adachi and Shimamura sit together, rubbing their hands in a playful ritual, hoping to be in the same class again.

stories.title: Summer at Your Side
stories.description: Adachi and Shimamura walk along the beach at sunset during their last summer vacation of high school. A quiet, tender moment of connection.

stories.title: A Question Befitting the Ruler of the World
stories.description: Shimamura and Adachi create quiz questions for each other to test how well they know one another. But when Adachi's questions turn out to be far too deep, Shimamura resorts to dancing to escape.

stories.title: Written By Adachi Sakura
stories.description: A story within a story. Adachi's mother—a novelist—struggles with her manuscript about two sisters on a dying planet. A meditation on writing, loss, and the impossible distance between people.

stories.title: A Future Like This Exists, Too
stories.description: A glimpse into a possible future—Adachi and Shimamura as parents, with two daughters and Yashiro, sharing a quiet moment at a donut shop.

stories.title: Akakura
stories.description: Shimamura wakes up in Adachi's body. A body-swap story that explores how deeply they understand each other—and how much they still have to learn.

stories.title: Aosagi
stories.description: Adachi wakes up in Shimamura's body. A body-swap story from the other side—navigating Shimamura's family, her mother's teasing, and the ache of being separated from the person she loves most.

stories.title: Looking Back Together
stories.description: Adachi and Shimamura watch old video clips of their high school days in a mysterious room. A nostalgic, tender moment of reflection on how much they've changed—and how much they haven't.

stories.title: The Dazzling Morning Sun that Melts Me
stories.description: Adachi wakes up early and reflects on her life with Shimamura. A quiet, tender morning moment filled with love, vulnerability, and the simple joy of making breakfast.
Shimamura reflects on their truant life in the gym—the stagnation, the silence, and the unexpected moment Adachi suggests playing ping-pong.

stories.title: 0.00000000000000000 Tarumi and Shimamura
stories.description: Tarumi reflects on her childhood friendship with Shimamura—how they grew apart, and the lingering regret that follows. A bittersweet look at the distance between people.

stories.title: Dog Sakura
stories.description: Adachi gets jealous of a pug. Shimamura decides to treat her like a dog for the day—and Adachi, true to form, goes along with it.

stories.title: I Cannot Be Measured
stories.description: Shimamura worries she's gained weight, but there's no scale in their apartment. Adachi's solution? A bridal carry—warm, earnest, and utterly perfect.

stories.title: In the Palm of My Hand
stories.description: Adachi wanders into Animate and finds Shimamura merchandise. Her immediate reaction? Wanting to buy it all—and swearing to become rich to claim every piece of Shimamura that ever exists.

stories.title: Like Water, an Ordinary Eternity / She Said 'I Hope So'
stories.description: A quiet afternoon at home. A memory of a childhood promise. Adachi and Shimamura share a moment of stillness, warmth, and the fragile hope that moments like this could last forever.

stories.title: Please Choose the Chocolate That Suits Me!
stories.description: A parallel universe story where Adachi never asked Shimamura to exchange chocolates on Valentine's Day. With Nagafuji's help and a boomerang detour, destiny still finds a way.

stories.title: Shimamura's Swimsuit
stories.description: Adachi admires Shimamura's swimsuit—and envies how close it gets to her. At the pool, she finally works up the courage to hug her, skin to skin, and discovers what it means to be that close.

stories.title: The Cave
stories.description: A dream. Adachi and Shimamura walk through a cave together, hand in hand. A quiet, surreal moment of connection that lingers even after waking.

stories.title: Too Long
stories.description: Shimamura reflects on Adachi's transparent, untouched nature. Adachi reflects on Shimamura as the ship that carried her to another world. Two sides of the same story—one measured, one burning.

stories.title: Sharing Warmth
stories.description: A cold winter day. Shimamura mentions she's cold, and Adachi rushes over to warm her up—hands, cheeks, and finally, her heart.

stories.title: Infinite Affection Points
stories.description: Shimamura wakes up with the ability to see people's affection points. She tests it on her family, Yashiro, Nagafuji—and then Adachi. A playful, heartwarming exploration of love, numbers, and what they really mean.

stories.title: The Scenery of the Park, Then and Now
stories.description: A date at the park after school—then, years later, a date at the same park after work. A story of growing up, staying together, and the quiet beauty of choosing each other every day.

stories.title: Toward the Fading Twilight
stories.description: Shimamura finishes work early and walks home through the sunset. Yashiro appears beside her in a boar mascot suit, sharing orange juice and a quiet moment before they part ways.

stories.title: From Me to You: Adachi's Letter
stories.description: Adachi writes her first letter to Shimamura—reflecting on their high school days, the warmth of the gym, and the love that has grown between them.

stories.title: From Me to You: Shimamura's Letter
stories.description: Shimamura writes back to Adachi—sharing memories of first impressions, the beauty of their shared life, and the quiet joy of loving someone.

stories.title: A Fun Little Quiz Show
stories.description: Adachi answers a quiz written by Shimamura—three questions about herself, her habits, and how Shimamura sees her. A playful, introspective look into Adachi's mind.

stories.title: Sharing Winter
stories.description: A quiet winter morning. Adachi and Shimamura share tea, warmth, and the simple joy of doing nothing together.

stories.title: A Pleasant Holiday Under the Sun
stories.description: Christmas Eve. Adachi wears a China dress—a remnant of her student days—and waits for Shimamura to come home. A warm, tender moment of reunion.

stories.title: Perfection That Feels Unstable
stories.description: Hino escapes a family gathering and meets a mysterious, beautiful woman in the garden. Later, Nagafuji arrives with a massive backpack—just as chaotic and familiar as always.

stories.title: Umi Meets Adachi
stories.description: A chance encounter at a Chinese restaurant. Umi, the narrator, meets a girl in a China dress—reluctant, awkward, and strikingly beautiful. A brief, quiet moment of connection.

stories.title: Day Two of My Birthday
stories.description: The day before Shimamura's birthday. Adachi arrives early, determined not to forget this year—and announces it to everyone she meets. A playful, warm morning with a touch of destiny.

stories.title: The Mystery of the Shimamura Family
stories.description: Shimamura's father reflects on three strange things: Hougetsu's unusual cheerfulness, the glowing girl who won't leave, and his wife's carefree attitude. A playful glimpse into the Shimamura household.

stories.title: It was Yesterday, Apparently
stories.description: Shimamura's birthday passed unnoticed again. Yashiro leads her to Adachi, and they share a quiet moment of forgetting—and remembering—together.

stories.title: My Mother was There
stories.description: Adachi returns from the bath to find the room dark and Shimamura pretending to sleep. A quiet, intimate moment where she considers kissing her—and sings a lullaby she didn't know she remembered.

stories.title: The Fake Sleep Prank
stories.description: Shimamura pretends to be asleep to see what Adachi will do. What she gets is a lullaby—and a moment of quiet, unspoken love.
